cdc_measles_cases.long_name: CDC Weekly Measles Case Counts - National Level
cdc_measles_cases.short_description: Weekly count of reported measles cases in the United States from CDC.
cdc_measles_cases.long_description: This dataset provides weekly counts of measles cases reported to the CDC at the national level. Data is updated weekly and represents confirmed and probable measles cases reported by state and local health departments. The case counts reflect the week ending date (Saturday) following epiweek conventions.
cdc_measles_cases.statement: In the United States, {value} measles cases were reported for the week ending {time}.

cdc_measles.name: CDC Measles Cases and Outbreaks
cdc_measles.organization: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
cdc_measles.description: The CDC Measles Cases and Outbreaks surveillance system tracks confirmed and probable measles cases reported to CDC by state and local health departments. Data includes weekly national case counts and outbreak information. Measles became a nationally notifiable disease in 1912, and since 2000 (when measles was declared eliminated in the U.S.), CDC has continued enhanced surveillance to detect and respond to imported cases and outbreaks. Case definitions follow the Council of State and Territorial Epidemiologists (CSTE) criteria requiring clinical symptoms and either laboratory confirmation or epidemiological linkage to a confirmed case.
cdc_measles.restrictions: Public domain. CDC data is generally not subject to copyright restrictions.

wapo_schools.name: Washington Post School Vaccination Data
wapo_schools.description: School-level vaccination and exemption rate data compiled by The Washington Post. The dataset includes vaccination coverage rates, exemption rates by type (medical, religious, personal belief), and school characteristics for schools across multiple states. Data was compiled from state-level reports and public records requests to support reporting on vaccination coverage gaps and potential outbreak risks.
wapo_schools.restrictions: CC BY 4.0. Attribution required for reuse. Cite The Washington Post.
